Part B.key_evidence: The cutouts show heavily corrupted/striped imagery with strong horizontal banding and a binary-looking template, not a clean PSF source. FWHM is large (6.81 px), classtar is mediocre (0.554), and the difference image lacks a clean localized residual. ndethist=1 with ncovhist=964 indicates a single detection at a frequently-observed position.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: The image artifacts (banding, masked template, no coherent point-source residual) strongly suggest this is a bogus detection from a bad subtraction or detector/readout artifact rather than a genuine astrophysical transient.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An alternative would be a real faint variable near the PS1 stellar source (sgscore1=0.95, distpsnr1=2.94"), but the poor image quality, large FWHM, and lack of a clean PSF-like residual make this unlikely to be trustworthy.
